prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a tidy row of evenly spaced empty buckets sits on a conveyor while a cheerful flock of irregular glowing fireflies arrives in unpredictable bursts, some buckets overflowing and many sitting empty, one firefly hovering exactly between two buckets unsure where to land. Style friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, warm tones, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Evenly spaced buckets on a conveyor try to catch irregularly arriving glowing fireflies, with some buckets overflowing and others empty, dramatizing how a fixed time grid mismatches real event timing.
caption: When the timing itself is the signal, forcing events into evenly spaced bins spills the very information you came to measure.

after_heading: 2. The Hawkes Process: Self-Excitation and Branching
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a single toppling domino sets off a branching fan of more dominoes, each fallen domino sparking two or three new little dominoes that lean and topple in turn, the cascade fading gently into calm upright dominoes far away. An expressive first domino looks slightly surprised at the chain reaction it started. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, warm tones. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: One toppling domino triggers a branching cascade of further dominoes that gradually fades to calm, illustrating how each Hawkes event raises the chance of more events before excitation decays.
caption: A Hawkes process is self exciting: every event nudges up the odds of the next, so one arrival can branch into a whole cascade that slowly settles back down.

after_heading: 3. Neural Temporal Point Processes: Learned Intensities
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a friendly robot conductor stands before a soft glowing wave that rises and falls, raising its baton to make the wave swell just before each new firefly pops into existence above it, then letting the wave sag in the quiet gaps. The robot reads the past fireflies trailing behind it to decide how high to lift the next swell. Style friendly like an XKCD panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, warm tones. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot conductor raises a baton to swell a glowing intensity wave just before each new firefly event appears, illustrating a neural temporal point process learning a flexible conditional intensity from event history.
caption: A neural point process learns to shape the intensity curve from history alone, swelling expectation right before events and relaxing it through the quiet stretches.

prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: several runners on a track, some have crossed a glowing finish ribbon while others are frozen mid stride behind a soft fog wall that marks where the camera stopped recording, their finish times still unknown. A cheerful observer with a stopwatch shrugs warmly at the foggy runners, knowing only that they had not finished yet. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, warm tones. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Some runners cross a finish ribbon while others are frozen mid stride behind a fog wall marking the end of observation, illustrating censored survival data where the event time is only known to exceed a cutoff.
caption: Censoring means you stopped watching before some events happened: you do not know their finish time, only that it was still ahead of the line.

prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cluster of little island nodes connected by glowing rope bridges, where some bridges are fading away while brand new bridges sprout between other islands, all in the same scene. A small expressive courier figure pauses mid step as the bridge it intended to cross dissolves, while a fresh bridge appears toward a different island. Style friendly like an XKCD panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, warm tones. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Island nodes linked by glowing bridges that appear and dissolve over time while a courier reroutes, illustrating a dynamic graph where edges form and vanish so message passing must account for when connections existed.
caption: On a temporal graph the connections themselves come and go, so a model has to ask not just who is linked but when the link was alive.

after_heading: 1. From Event Logs to Process Models: The Gap Between Design and Reality
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a neat straight paved garden path laid out by a proud planner, while the actual visitors have worn a tangle of muddy shortcut trails across the grass that loop, backtrack, and skip stones entirely, ignoring the tidy path. A puzzled planner stands on the empty official path watching the crowd take the messy desire lines. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, gentle dry humor, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, warm tones. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A planner stands on a tidy straight paved path while crowds wear muddy looping shortcut trails across the grass, illustrating the gap between the designed process model and the messy real behavior recovered from event logs.
caption: Process discovery reveals the worn shortcut trails people actually walk, which rarely match the clean path the process was designed to follow.
